questions.question: Why isn't Whisper-style transcription enough for audio products?
options: Whisper is too slow for production

Recognition is not reasoning — questions about instruments, speaker emotion, or "what happened at minute 3" need audio UNDERSTANDING; a transcript discards timing, speakers, emotion, music, and environmental sound
Transcription requires internet access
Whisper only supports English
questions.explanation: The cascade route (Whisper transcribes, LLM reasons over text) works for pure speech but fails on music, environmental audio, multi-speaker overlap, and emotion — everything acoustic the transcript throws away.

questions.question: How is a log-Mel spectrogram computed?
options: Direct FFT of the whole file at once

Resample to 16kHz, STFT with 25ms windows and 10ms hop, take FFT magnitudes, apply ~80 Mel filter banks (perceptual frequency warp), then log-compress — yielding a (T, 80) array, e.g. (3000, 80) for 30 seconds
Run the waveform through a CNN classifier
Convert audio to MIDI notes
questions.explanation: Every audio encoder in the arc starts from this same feature. Whisper's 12-layer ViT-style encoder then processes it as a sequence of time frames, one hidden vector per frame.

questions.question: Why does Audio Flamingo 3 concatenate Whisper AND BEATs features (AF-Whisper)?
options: To double the parameter count for marketing

Whisper was trained on speech-dominant data and is weaker on music/environmental audio; BEATs (self-supervised on AudioSet) captures those better — Whisper carries the linguistic signal, BEATs the acoustic signal
BEATs is required by the license
Whisper cannot output embeddings
questions.explanation: Encoder choice tracks the audio distribution: Whisper for speech, BEATs for music and sounds, the hybrid when the product must handle both.

questions.question: What is the standard pattern for attaching an audio encoder to an LLM?
options: Retrain everything from scratch
Frozen Whisper-class encoder → trainable audio Q-former (N learnable queries cross-attending to spectrogram frames) → LLM frozen or lightly tuned
Pipe transcripts through the tokenizer
Average all encoder outputs into one vector
questions.explanation: Same Q-former pattern as BLIP-2 for vision (Phase 12 · 03), applied to audio: the small trainable bridge translates encoder features into tokens the LLM can consume.

questions.question: Why does end-to-end audio-LLM training beat the cascade for reasoning?
options: It is cheaper to train
Audio tokens flow directly into the LLM, preserving acoustic information (emotion, speaker identity, environment) that transcription destroys — at the cost of needing new audio-text instruction data
Cascades cannot run on GPUs
End-to-end models skip the encoder
questions.explanation: Qwen-Audio, SALMONN, LTU, and AF3 progressively built this stack: Whisper-class encoders, Q-former bridges, audio instruction tuning, and finally chain-of-thought reasoning over audio.
